Astragalus is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the legume family (Fabaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Astragalus is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and improve overall health.
